ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for casarecce specifically; 'pasta dish' (FOODON_00002473) is the closest defensible parent match. Courgette-basil-pesto casarecce is a specific preparation not individually coded in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pasta, cooked) is the closest defensible parent match. No specific code exists for casarecce with pesto and vegetables in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for casarecce pasta dish. SNOMED CT does not enumerate individual pasta preparations at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant plate of Courgette and Basil Casarecce weighs approximately 350 g and provides around 680 kcal, making it a moderate-to-high calorie single-dish meal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pasta, cooked, enriched, without added salt (SR Legacy #20420) and component ingredients including basil pesto, courgette, Parmesan, and sugar snap peas
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for casarecce with basil pesto, courgette, sugar snap peas, gremolata, and Parmesan as a composite dish. Nutrient values were derived by weighted-ingredient estimation using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked pasta (FDC #20420), courgette/zucchini cooked (FDC #11478), sugar snap peas (FDC #11300), Parmesan cheese (FDC #01032), and olive oil (FDC #04053), cross-referenced against the input record values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7 from GrabFood). Input values were retained where consistent with USDA component estimates; minor rounding applied. Singapore HPB Foodo database does not list this specific Western pasta preparation. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was not used as it does not correspond to an approved nutrient database entry for this dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists in the University of Sydney GI Database for casarecce pasta specifically. GI of 45 is estimated from the closest analogous approved-source entry: 'Pasta, white, boiled' (GI approximately 45–50, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021, Am J Clin Nutr 114(5):1625–1632). Casarecce is a twisted short-cut durum wheat semolina pasta with a dense structure similar to other short-cut white pasta forms. The pesto sauce and vegetable components do not materially raise GI. GL per serving calculated as: GI (45) × available carbohydrate per serving (18.6 g/100g × 350 g / 100) = 45 × 65.1 / 100 = 29.3, rounded to 29.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 271 mg/100g (949 mg per 350 g serving) approaches the 1000 mg per-meal threshold recommended for hypertensive individuals; Parmesan cheese is the primary sodium contributor.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should request reduced Parmesan and ask for the dish to be prepared with minimal added salt; the potassium-rich courgette, sugar snap peas, and parsley in the gremolata partially offset sodium load, but portion control to 200–250 g is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined durum wheat casarecce contributes 18.6 g carbohydrate/100g with only 2.0 g dietary fibre/100g; a 350 g serving delivers approximately 65 g total carbohydrate, which may cause post-prandial glucose elevation despite the low-to-medium GI (~45) of the pasta.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit the portion to 200–250 g, request wholemeal or legume-based pasta to increase fibre and lower GI, and pair the dish with additional non-starchy vegetables to moderate the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Extra-virgin olive oil in the pesto contributes heart-healthy monounsaturated fatty acids (~5.2 g/100g); saturated fat is moderate at 3.4 g/100g and trans fat is negligible at 0.1 g/100g; the Mediterranean dietary pattern of this dish is broadly consistent with cardiovascular risk reduction.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is broadly suitable for individuals with cardiovascular disease when consumed in moderate portions (250–300 g); sodium content (271 mg/100g) warrants attention — request reduced Parmesan and no added salt to keep the per-meal sodium below 800 mg.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Extra-virgin olive oil provides oleocanthal with COX-inhibiting anti-inflammatory properties; basil, garlic, and pine nuts contribute polyphenols and vitamin E; chilli is a nightshade ingredient that may aggravate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshades should request the dish without chilli; the olive-oil-based pesto and vegetable components are otherwise anti-inflammatory and well-tolerated.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Courgette and Basil Casarecce is a Western Italian-inspired pasta dish served in Singapore restaurants and available via food delivery platforms. It features casarecce — a short, twisted durum wheat semolina pasta with a scroll-like shape that holds sauces well — tossed with a vibrant fresh basil pesto made from basil leaves, extra-virgin olive oil, pine nuts, garlic, and Parmesan cheese. The dish is finished with sliced courgette (zucchini), crisp sugar snap peas, a zesty gremolata of lemon zest, parsley, and garlic, a hint of fresh chilli, and additional grated Parmesan. The texture is al dente with a creamy, herbaceous coating; the flavour profile is bright, grassy, and lightly nutty with citrus and mild heat. Nutritionally, a 350 g restaurant serving provides approximately 680 kcal, 25 g protein, 38 g fat (predominantly heart-healthy MUFAs from olive oil), and 65 g carbohydrate. Sodium is moderate at 271 mg/100g (949 mg per serving), primarily from Parmesan. The dish is vegetarian but not vegan or gluten-free. It contains tree nut allergens (pine nuts) and dairy (Parmesan). The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low) based on the white pasta base, and the glycemic load per serving is approximately 29.4, which is high in absolute terms due to the large carbohydrate mass. Clinically, the dish is suitable for most adults but requires portion moderation for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The Mediterranean-style olive oil base supports cardiovascular health, while the nightshade ingredient (chilli) may be a concern for rheumatoid arthritis sufferers.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific composite dish. Nutrient values were estimated by weighted-ingredient composition using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the component ingredients, cross-referenced against the input record (ai_generated, confidence 0.7). The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for white pasta. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries (cooked pasta FDC #20420, courgette FDC #11478, sugar snap peas FDC #11300, Parmesan FDC #01032, olive oil FDC #04053) because no single approved-source entry exists for this composite dish. Input values (ai_generated, GrabFood, confidence 0.7) were retained where consistent with USDA component estimates. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient breakdown for this specific composite restaurant preparation is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. 3. potassium_mg (220 mg/100g) is an estimate based on USDA component values for courgette, sugar snap peas, and parsley; not directly measured. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (5.2 g/100g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.6 g/100g) are estimated from USDA olive oil and pine nut fatty acid profiles proportional to estimated pesto content. 5. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate for white pasta (boiled) from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021 Am J Clin Nutr; no direct GI measurement exists for casarecce. 6. The input ethnic_tags included 'Cake' and 'Burger' which are clearly erroneous for this dish and have been removed. 7. Halal status is unconfirmed — Parmesan cheese may use animal rennet; dish is not certified halal. 8. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was listed as HPB Singapore but does not correspond to the official HPB Foodo nutrient database and was not used for nutrient values. 9. Ontology match types set to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 as no exact code exists for this specific preparation.
